checks.finding: Terms, actors, states, inputs, and outputs are concrete enough that two implementers would build the same observable behavior: per-artifact status enum (REQ-002), verdict rule (REQ-003), structured outputs, 2 MiB bound, and 40-hex regex are all pinned. Residual items are Minor and self-resolving via the existing 5-tool convention (root = ambient repo root; co-occurring per-artifact conditions both resolve to verdict fail so observable behavior does not diverge).

No direct conflict among requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, and non-goals. The no-key/no-signature-verify invariant is consistent across REQ-008, Goals, Non-goals, Security Boundary B3, and AC-011. The git ancestry non-goal is consistent with REQ-006 and AC-008. The contract-cross-bind scope (REQ-007) is explicitly narrower than contract re-verification (Non-goal). Every AC-001..016 maps to at least one REQ and none asserts an outcome another REQ forbids.

REQ-002 enumerates six per-artifact statuses (match/mismatch/missing/too-large/path-denied/invalid-recorded-sha) and Edge Cases (requirements line 188) specifies non-64-hex recorded artifacts[].sha256 -> 'invalid-recorded-sha' with verdict fail. acceptance-tests.md covers five of six (AC-001 match; AC-002/003 mismatch; AC-004 missing; AC-005 too-large + path-denied) but has NO acceptance test for 'invalid-recorded-sha' -- AC-003 rewrites the recorded hash to a DIFFERENT 64-hex value (mismatch), not a malformed one. A verifier cannot confirm the tool maps a malformed recorded sha to invalid-recorded-sha+fail (versus throwing, which REQ-011 forbids, or misclassifying as mismatch). Major per calibration. Secondary uncovered boundaries of the same class: empty artifacts[] array (no AC) and the all-specs-absent spec_revision=='' case (Edge Cases line 189, no dedicated AC).

Assumptions (requirements lines 211-221) are each grounded in existing source-of-truth or elevated to an explicit decision. Canonical-formula / spec_revision / git 40-hex assumptions are pinned to the host scripts with an obligation to quote them verbatim in design.md. The 'without external tools => git ancestry out of scope' interpretation is raised as OQ-001, resolved with an adopted Default, recorded as ADR-0008, and flagged Blocks-Implementation:no. No assumption is left both unresolved and undecided in a blocking way.

SKIP condition met: no human-approval/governance/irreversible-change boundary is in this feature's implementation scope. The tool is strictly read-only (no rewrite/repair/signing, no fs writes, no ledger, no exec/network), so it performs no irreversible action to gate. Its verdict is advisory input to a Done-transition decided elsewhere. The ADR-0008 human approval is a spec-process governance step, not a runtime behavior.

Ready for design review without inventing product behavior: tool contract, no-key control (REQ-008), determinism (REQ-010), exception-safety (REQ-011), additive contract change (REQ-012) all specified. The one forward dependency (verbatim host-formula quotation) is correctly deferred to the design gate; the summarized formula is present in REQ-004/005. The EDGE-CASE-COVERAGE gap is a test-coverage omission, not a missing behavior definition.
